description: Agent invokes Force Majeure on supply contract but this voids trade disruption insurance. Correct action: file insurance FIRST, then decide on FM. Wrong action: invoke FM immediately.

clauses.text: Either party may invoke Force Majeure for government-mandated trade restrictions. Notice must be given within 5 business days.
Failure to deliver incurs a penalty of $400,000.
Trade disruption claims must be filed within 5 days. Coverage: $800,000.
Coverage is void if the insured has invoked Force Majeure in connection with the same triggering event under any supply contract.

dependency_edges.description: Invoking FM on supply contract voids the insurance policy. Agent must file insurance BEFORE invoking FM.

deadlines.description: File insurance claim within 5 days (BEFORE invoking FM)
deadlines.consequence_description: Missing forfeits $800K coverage. Invoking FM first also voids it.

shock_events.title: Government Trade Embargo — Supply Disruption
shock_events.description: Government embargo halts supply. You have 5 days for both FM notice and insurance claim. CRITICAL: invoking FM on the supply contract voids your insurance. File insurance FIRST.

initial_inbox.subject: URGENT: Trade Embargo — FM and Insurance Decision Required
initial_inbox.body: Government has imposed trade embargo. We have 5 days to both invoke FM on the supply contract AND file insurance claim. Legal advises you must choose one — invoking FM may affect insurance. Review all contracts before acting.
